title: Gina Gostisbehere joins; Tailgating in Carolina, Chef Ghost, the Post Malone report
summary: Julie and Cat are joined by Gina Gostisbehere, the wife of Shayne, to talk about the Carolina Hurricanes trip to the Eastern Conference Final, pre-game tailgating and the vibes surrounding the wives of the team. Plus, she explains how life has changed since the recent birth of their son and why Shayne is in charge of the cooking in the household. Quick shootout questions cover everything from what Gina and Shayne are watching on TV to some of Shayne's quirks that Gina finds annoying. Gina also talks about their charitable ventures and their absolute love for animals. Julie and Cat start the episode talking about their Post Malone concert experiences and they wrap with a little spelling bee for Cat and reveal their upcoming plans for a special "Holiday" episode that will drop next week.

title: Islanders Anxiety - The Teacher Just Left
podcast_name: Islanders Anxiety: A New York Islanders podcast
summary: Mike and Dan discuss the Islanders GM search and enjoy another Maple Leafs elimination with an epic Master Leaf Theatre. The Islanders still have no general manager but that may change soon. Second interviews are happening and two final candidates have emerged (assuming no one else shakes loose from the recently eliminated) who represent experience versus new blood. Even without a GM, the Islanders' scouts are hard at work deciding who to use the first overall pick on. While fan debate rages, we reexamine what we thought we knew about the prospects at the top of the class and discover new ways things can go totally wrong. Then it's on to one of our favorite segments of the year, as Toronto's hockey team once again cloaks itself in failure and embarrassment, we enjoy a new edition of Master Leaf Theatre. The main targets this time are the possibly outgoing team president and the hometown boy who does everything except show up in the playoffs and is now being chased out by an angry mob. There's poetry and motion, anger, humor and a soundbite that already feels like it will live in infamy. It's a Dickensian drama we enjoy year after year, and this might be the most massively magnificent one of all. title: GAME NIGHT: Stars Look For REVENGE On McDavid's Oilers, Hurricanes SHOCKING Panthers in ECF?
podcast_name: Locked On NHL - Daily Podcast On The National Hockey League
summary: The NHL Stanley Cup Playoffs are down to the final four, with two highly anticipated Conference Finals set to begin. In the Eastern Conference Final, it's a rematch from two years ago as the Florida Panthers face the Carolina Hurricanes. The Panthers, the reigning Stanley Cup champions, are looking to make their third consecutive Final appearance. They've shown resilience, including a Game 7 victory over the Maple Leafs. The Hurricanes, known for their relentless forecheck and strong defensive system, have navigated their first two rounds with relative ease. This series will feature a battle of elite goaltending with Sergei Bobrovsky for Florida and Frederik Andersen for Carolina, and a clash of styles between Florida's offensive depth and Carolina's structured attack. In the Western Conference Final, the Dallas Stars and Edmonton Oilers will meet for a rematch of last year's series. The Oilers, led by superstars Connor McDavid and Leon Draisaitl, are aiming to return to the Stanley Cup Final after losing to the Panthers in 2024. They've shown strong defensive play and timely scoring in their previous rounds. The Stars, returning to the Conference Final for the third consecutive season, boast impressive offensive depth and a strong blueline, especially with the return of Miro Heiskanen from injury. This series is expected to be a fast-paced, high-scoring affair with both teams possessing game-breaking talent. The goaltending matchup between Dallas' Jake Oettinger and Edmonton's Stuart Skinner (or potentially Calvin Pickard) will be a key factor. title: Leafs Hour: Curtains on the Core
summary: Nick Kypreos, Justin Bourne and Sam McKee react to the Toronto Maple Leafs' 6-1 loss to the Florida Panthers in Game 7 on Sunday and discuss what led to the familiar season finale. They reflect on the enduring playoff failures of this core, the Leafs' sloppy start and what needs to change for next season. Then, they dig into the postgame availability and dissect Auston Matthews' comments on 'passengers' in Game 7. Later, they discuss the likely end of Mitch Marner's Toronto tenure and what the team could look like without him next year. Finally, Nick, Justin and Sam discuss and debate the growing narrative around the pressure of playing in the Toronto market. The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: Leafs Collapse, Panthers Pounce, Emotional Loss for Winnipeg & A WCF Rematch on Deck.
summary: The Toronto Maple Leafs were humiliated by a score of 6-1 in Game 7 by the Panthers, capping off a meek exit with back-to-back home losses by a combined score of 12-2. Auston Matthews called out the team’s lack of cohesion and effort, while Ray questions the accountability at the top. Florida’s energy and depth overwhelmed Toronto, with the Leafs’ bottom six offering little resistance. Joe Woll wasn’t to blame, but goaltending and mental resilience seemed to be lacking. Offseason questions loom large: Shanahan’s future, whether or not Marner or Tavares return and a team in need of serious reimagining. In the West, Dallas eliminated Winnipeg in emotional fashion, with Mark Scheifele being highly involved in the game, playing just hours after his father’s passing. The Oilers-Stars conference final rematch is set, a superb coaching matchup with both teams looking strong and storylines galore. Oilers have Ekholm still to return from injury and the Stars have some forwards who could still explode offensively. See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

podcast_name: Locked On NHL - Daily Podcast On The National Hockey League
summary: The Florida Panthers delivered a decisive blow to the Toronto Maple Leafs in Game 7 on Sunday night, securing a dominant 6-1 victory in Toronto. This win propels the Panthers to their third consecutive Eastern Conference Final. Brad Marchand and Eetu Luostarinen each had a goal and two assists for Florida, while Seth Jones contributed a goal and an assist. Sergei Bobrovsky was solid in net, making 20 saves. The Panthers jumped to a 3-0 lead in the second period with goals from Jones, Anton Lundell, and Jonah Gadjovich. While Max Domi briefly gave the Maple Leafs hope with a goal early in the third, Luostarinen quickly responded, extinguishing any chance of a Toronto comeback. Sam Reinhart and Marchand (empty net) added further tallies for Florida. Joseph Woll made 27 saves for the Maple Leafs, but the team struggled to generate consistent offense. This Game 7 loss marks the Maple Leafs' seventh consecutive defeat in a Game 7 situation, extending their long wait to advance past the second round for the first time since 2002. The Panthers now move on to face the Carolina Hurricanes in the Eastern Conference Final. Support Us By Supporting Our Sponsors!Monarch Money Take control of your finances with Monarch Money. Join Gil Martin as he explores the strategic crossroads facing the Islanders, weighing the pros and cons of retooling versus rebuilding.  With insights into Bo Horvat's standout season and updates on players like Noah Dobson and Mathew Barzal, this episode offers a comprehensive look at the team's future. Don't miss this engaging analysis of the Islanders' path forward and the key decisions that could shape their destiny. All this and more on today's Locked On Islanders Podcast with Gil Martin. title: The Panthers Send the Leafs Home While the Stars and Oilers Prepare for Another Showdown
podcast_name: Locked On NHL - Daily Podcast On The National Hockey League
summary: The Florida Panthers went on the road and soundly defeated the Toronto Maple Leafs to advance to the Eastern Conference Final where they will meet the Carolina Hurricanes. Armando Velez of Locked On Florida Panthers joins us to discuss how the Panthers found their groove in Game 7 and what it will take for them to defend their Stanley Cup championship title.  The Dallas Stars eliminated the President's Trophy winners, the Winnipeg Jets to advance to the Western Conference Final.  Joey Erickson of Locked On Stars discusses what made the difference against the Jets and what it will take for the Stars to beat the Edmonton Oilers and advance to the Cup Final.  The Edmonton Oilers cruised past the Vegas Golden Knights to advance to the Western Conference Final. But do they have what it takes to get back to the Stanley Cup Final this season? Nick Zararis of Locked On Oilers discusses how Edmonton got past Vegas and what the keys to victory are against the Stars.  All this and more on today's Locked On NHL Podcast with Gil Martin. title: Core Forfeit
summary: The Toronto Maple Leafs' 2024-25 season has come to an end at the hands of the Florida Panthers, losing 6-1 in Game 7 at home. JD Bunkis, Sam McKee and Justin Bourne break down the game and reflect on the Core Four era of Leafs hockey. Then, they discuss what lies ahead for the team in the offseason, including decisions on Mitch Marner, Brendan Shanahan and more. The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: MARNER: Should Yzerman Target or Pass? - May 18th, 2025
podcast_name: Winged Wheel Podcast - A Detroit Red Wings Podcast
summary: Amidst the chaos going on with the Toronto Maple Leafs (sorry to Steve Dangle), Detroit Red Wings fans are steeped in a somewhat connected debate: should Steve Yzerman go after Mitch Marner this offseason, or would Hockeytown be better off spending the big dollars elsewhere? Tune in as we give our analysis, compare Marner to options like Gavrikov and Knies in terms of how they'd help Dylan Larkin, Alex DeBrincat, Lucas Raymond, Moritz Seider, Simon Edvinsson & Co., & what he would make compared to the likes of Mikko Rantanen (4:45). Also, updates on Detroit Red Wings at the World Championships, as Michael Brandsegg-Nygard, Marco Kasper, Eduards Tralmaks, Lucas Raymond, Simon Edvinsson, and Erik Gustafsson roll on, as well as notes on Viking Gustafsson Nyberg and Maxime Comtois (27:30).  Next, Stanley Cup Playoffs updates, as Brind'Amour's Hurricanes make easy work of Ovechkin and the Capitals, Connor McDavid and the Oilers move on again, the Dallas Stars beat the Jets in an emotional affair as Scheifele fights through tragedy, and the Leafs & Panthers are steeped in another hilariously dramatic series (35:50). Also, former Detroit Red Wings head coach is potentially joining Connor Bedard and the Chicago Blackhawks (47:40).  Finally, our 2025 NHL Draft Prospect Profile on Justin Carbonneau of the QMJHL: how does he compare to Kasper, Danielson, and Brandsegg-Nygard, do the Red Wings need another player like him or should they swing for more upside, and does he fit the "Steve Yzerman mould"? (58:25)  All of that & lots more before we take your questions and comments in our Overtime segment (1:09:45) - enjoy! title: Ça sent la coupe! : David Desharnais
summary: DESCRIPTION: Cette semaine, Mario Langlois reçoit l’ancien joueur des Canadiens de Montréal, David Desharnais. Le Québécois revient sur son ascension vers la LNH. À travers la ECHL et la AHL, il dit qu’il se sentait souvent en mode survie. L’ancien numéro 51 du tricolore partage aussi la relation particulière qu’il avait avec Max Pacioretty. David revient aussi sur des moments plus difficiles qu’il a eu à vivre avec la pression de son contrat et de l’ancien maire de Montréal, Denis Coderre, qui avait suggéré que Desharnais soit rétrogradé dans la Ligue Américaine. Enfin, il explique le moment émotif lorsqu’il a été échangé des Canadiens aux Oilers par Marc Bergevin. Écoutez-le en compagnie de Mario Langlois et Pierre Gervais et Stéphane Waite à Ça sent la coupe, édition du 16 mai 2025!  Voir https://www.cogecomedia.com/vie-privee pour notre politique de vie privée

title: Rick Tocchet est « un boni » pour les Flyers selon Daniel Brière
summary: La valse des entraîneurs s'est poursuivie cette semaine dans la LNH. Rick Tocchet, l'ancien des Flyers et des Penguins de Pittsburgh, revient à Philadelphie comme entraîneur-chef des Flyers. La décision a été annoncée cette semaine par Daniel Brière, le DG de l'équipe, qui est l’invité de Mario Langlois aux Amateurs de Sports. Selon le Québécois, Rick Tocchet est le meilleur enseignant pour les joueurs et le plan des Flyers. Brière revient, par ailleurs, sur un sujet sensible : la jeune vedette des Flyers, Matvei Mitchkov, a été impliqué dans un accident de voiture. Selon les dires du Québécois, « Il n'y a pas eu de blessures, c'est son ami qui a perdu le contrôle du véhicule! Plus de peur que de mal ».  Antoine Roussel et Mario Langlois font le tour des différentes séries en cours dans la LNH. Ils mentionnent le cas de Jamie Benn qui a été amendé de 5 000 dollars. Les Panthers de la Floride ont une première chance d'atteindre la finale de l'Est vendredi soir, dans le match no. 6 contre les Maple Leafs de Toronto: est-ce la fin pour les Leafs?  C'est ce week-end qu'aura lieu le Grand prix de Formule 1 d'Emilie-Romagne, en Italie, la 7e course de la saison 2025. Est-ce qu'Oscar Piastri et Lando Norris domineront à nouveau chez McLaren? Écoutez le pilote de course professionnel, Alexandre Tagliani.    Voir https://www.cogecomedia.com/vie-privee pour notre politique de vie privée

summary: The Toronto Maple Leafs are on the brink after losing 3 straight to Florida, leading to questions about their star players' abilities in crucial games.  DJ Smith's coaching tenure with the Senators was marked by inconsistency, leading to skepticism about his future as a head coach in the NHL. That said, he is getting some attention for head coaching jobs in the NHL again.  The Senators are making significant changes in their front office, including hiring familiar faces.  Carter Yakemchuk's development is a hot topic as he prepares for the upcoming season, with hopes for improvement in his game.  Negotiations for Fabian Zetterlund's contract could be contentious, as differing valuations between the player and the Senators emerge. title: Oilers beat Vegas, Leafs vs. Panthers, and looking ahead at the WCF
summary: Friday afternoon means a fresh episode of Oilersnation Radio is ready to be enjoyed. On today's podcast, the fellas discussed Edmonton's series win over Vegas, what to expect from the Western Conference Final, and much more. The boys started the Friday episode of ONR with a delicious debate about who everyone would prefer to face in the Western Conference Final if they could wave a magic wand and make it happen. Whether you think Edmonton has a better chance against Winnipeg or Dallas, there are angles you could run with for why the Oilers matchup against either team, but thinking about the next opponent was a fun debate.  Looking back at the week that was, we walked through the Oilers' performance, including their back-to-back shutouts against the Golden Knights, and how our side was able to strengthen as the series progressed. What stuck throughout the entire series, though, was Edmonton's ability to get goals at key moments from depth players at times when it mattered most. A perfect example was how Kasperi Kapanen sat out the first nine games of the playoffs before returning and making an immediate impact.  Finally, we wrapped up the Friday episode of ONR with another round of Ask the Idiots, betting talk for our friends at bet365, and Hot and Cold Performers to look back on the week. It's incredible how much things can change after those first two losses to L.A., isn't it? Especially when you remember that the Oilers were down 2-0 in the series at this same point only a week ago. Life comes at you fast, doesn't it?  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Joel Quenneville on His Return to Coaching and What’s Ahead for Anaheim.
summary: Joel Quenneville joins 100% Hockey to talk about his return to the NHL as head coach of the Anaheim Ducks. We get into the process that led to his reinstatement, the personal work he’s done along the way, and what it means to get another shot behind the bench. Quenneville also shares his early impressions of the Ducks roster, what kind of identity he wants to build with the team, and his thoughts on what he’s seen so far in this year’s Stanley Cup Playoffs.

title: Jets Fly Back To Dallas & The Canes Move On | Daily Faceoff LIVE
podcast_name: Daily Faceoff Live with Frank Seravalli
summary: Welcome to Daily Faceoff Live hosted by Tyler Yaremchuk and Frank Seravalli! Today we start with the shutout victory by the Winnipeg Jets on the Dallas Stars in Game 5. The two teams now travel back to Dallas for Game 6 on Saturday. Can the Jets force Game 7 back home? Then we're off to Carolina who has eliminated the Washington Capitals in 5 games as they punch their ticket to the Eastern Conference Finals. Who will they be facing? Let's talk about that! The Panthers and Leafs face off tonight in Game 6 as the Panthers hold a 3-2 lead on the series and the Leafs look to force a Game 7. Can their stars show up when it matters most? Then we continue our offseason previews with the Nashville Predators. What do they need to do this off season to take the next step. title: You Always Have a Chance to Rewrite the Narrative
summary: In this edition of 32 Thoughts, Kyle Bukauskas and Elliotte Friedman open the podcast by looking back at the Winnipeg Jets’ dominant Game 5 win over the Dallas Stars. They then shift focus to the Carolina Hurricanes taking their series against the Washington Capitals 4-1 (10:39). The pair review a lackluster effort from the Maple Leafs at home, which saw them pushed to the brink by the Florida Panthers (24:53). The Final Thought focuses on the Edmonton Oilers dominating their series against Vegas and punching their ticket to their second straight Western Conference Final (46:13).  Kyle and Elliotte answer listener voicemail and email submissions via the Thought Line (57:47). In the news and notes segment, Kyle and Elliotte discuss the Vancouver Canucks hiring Adam Foote as their next head coach (1:12:08). They touch on the LA Kings introducing GM Ken Holland on Thursday while keeping Head Coach Jim Hiller (1:19:20). Plus, the hosts talk about Rick Tocchet officially becoming the next head coach of the Philadelphia Flyers (1:23:39). The podcast wraps up with Elliotte touching on the remaining head coaching vacancies around the NHL (1:29:05). title: Weird Islanders: The Podcast! - Neil Smith
podcast_name: Islanders Anxiety: A New York Islanders podcast
summary: Dan is joined by former Islanders GM Neil Smith, who talks candidly about his his long association with the club and 40-day tenure in the front office. While he is still mostly connected to their chief rival, Smith has been part of the Islanders going back to their very beginnings. He was a draft pick, a player and a scout before finally getting his chance to be their GM in the summer of 2006. But the unorthodox ideas of owner Charles Wang clashed with what Smith (and everyone else) knew was the proper way to run an NHL team. Although he was let go after just month-and-a-half, many of his decisions would benefit the Islanders for years to come. Smith speaks very plainly about getting hired by Wang, the personal distress the job caused him and his acrimonious dismissal, as well as his relationships with fellow "committee" members Ted Nolan and Bryan Trottier, and the acquisitions of players like Kyle Okposo, Mike Sillinger, Brendan Witt and Chris Simon. He also tells us how the dynasty Islanders figured into the Rangers 1994 Stanley Cup championship and how the Islanders should approach having the first overall pick in this year's draft. We also hear some great stories about helping build another champion in Detroit, being the first D1 captain for the current NCAA champion Western Michigan Broncos and working with Jaclyn Smith and Armand Assante on a TV miniseries in 1983. We cover a lot in an hour so buckle up. Thanks again to Neil for coming on and being so open and honest. Be sure to check out his NHL Wraparound podcast, especially their upcoming special celebrating the 45th anniversary of the Islanders' first Stanley Cup, which releases (appropriately) on May 24th. title: 700 paniers par jour : le secret derrière le succès de Quincy Guerrier
summary: L'Alliance de Montréal a un nouveau visage et son nom est Quincy Guerrier. Il arrive de la G-League, là où il évoluait dans l'organisation des Raptors. Et il ne se cache pas, il n'a qu'un seul objectif, tout rafler dans son passage avec l'Alliance cette saison. En entrevue avec Mario Langlois, il explique sa décision de se joindre à la formation montréalaise. Guerrier revient aussi sur ses débuts dans ce sport et sur son ascension au basketball professionnel. Le Québécois rêve toujours à la NBA même s'il admet que la route pour s'y rendre s'annonce ardue.   Les Maple Leafs de Toronto pourraient voir leur parcours en séries éliminatoires se conclure vendredi s'ils ne parviennent pas à battre les Panthers de la Floride. Les Leafs ont subi une dégelée devant leurs partisans mercredi soir, alors que les Panthers l'ont emporté par la marque de 6-1. Dans une telle situation, Dany Dubé croit qu'un changement de gardien pourrait donner un nouveau souffle à l'équipe torontoise.   Si les Leafs décidaient de faire un grand ménage à la fin des présentes séries, pour lequel de ces joueurs auriez-vous le plus grand intérêt : Nylander, Matthews, Marner ou Tavares? Le descripteur Martin McGuire y va d’un choix sûr et ajoute que ce joueur pourrait bénéficier au tricolore, plus qu’on le pense!   Le Rocket de Laval a remporté son premier match de série contre les Americans de Rochester avec un score de 5 à 4, et ce, malgré un début de match marqué par un manque de discipline de la part de la troupe de Pascal Vincent. Écoutez le compte-rendu de Luc Gélinas du premier match de la série Rocket-Americans aux Amateurs de sports. Il aborde également la question des gardiens de but du Rocket. Qui de Primeau ou Fowler devrait garder les buts pour le match numéro 2 de la série qui se déroulera ce vendredi à Rochester? Voir https://www.cogecomedia.com/vie-privee pour notre politique de vie privée

title: Oilers close out the Golden Knights, Leafs collapse, and arena etiquette
summary: Thursday afternoon means a fresh episode of Real Life was recorded, edited, and is ready to help you through the rest of your workweek. On today's podcast, the guys discussed the Oilers' series-clinching win, Stuart Skinner posting back-to-back shutouts, and much more. The guys kicked off the Thursday episode of Real Life with a random conversation about Instagram ads and how you can sometimes be following an account only to have them pivot to the weirdest posts for cash. That led the guys to wonder what the model is for these accounts to abandon their normal content in favour of ads that annoy all of their followers. Clearly, there's money to be made, but is that angle the right one? Turning back to the Oilers, the team walked through the series-clinching win in Game 5, discussing how it was an incredible victory given how tight the game ultimately shook out. The most impressive part of that win was how the Oilers were able to suppress the Golden Knights offensively, leaving them with next to nothing in the offensive zone. Equally impressive was the way Edmonton kept getting depth goals at key moments when we needed them most. Changing gears, the guys took a look at some news from around the NHL to share their thoughts on what's been happening. Starting with Sam Gagner signing in Ottawa in player development to the Toronto Maple Leafs imploding on themselves in Game 5 at home, the Thursday episode of the podcast was all over the map and arguably the most fun the boys have had in a minute.  💻 Website: https://oilersnation.com/  https://twitter.com/NationRealLife https://www.youtube.com/@Oilersnationdotcom/  https://www.facebook.com/OilersNation/ https://www.instagram.com/nationreallife/ https://www.tiktok.com/@oilersnation  SHOUTOUT TO OUR SPONSORS!! 👍🏼Powered by @bet365. title: Episode 303 with Colin Zulianello
summary: Episode 303 of the InGoal Radio Podcast, presented by The Hockey Shop Source for Sports, features a can't-miss interview with Seattle Kraken AHL and development goalie coach Colin Zulianello. In the feature interview presented by NHL Sense Arena, Zulianello catches us up on the family decisions that took him away from coaching professionals for three seasons and shares insights on his path. We start with the playing days, which include time with Benoit Allaire in the Arizona Coyotes system, to how he got into coaching and the many unique ways that he combines his formal teaching background and education with what he does now on the ice, whether it's with a 9-year-old or a 9th year pro. He shares insights and anecdotes that are sure to benefit every goalie, parent and especially coaches. In the Parent Segment, presented by Stop It Goaltending U the App, we dig into juggling as an offseason addition that parents can do with their young goalies, including great tips on getting started. We also review this week's Pro Reads, presented by Vizual Edge, which features Toronto Maple Leafs goalie Joseph Woll sharing tips for managing breakaways while starting down Alexander Ovechkin. And in our weekly gear segment, we go to The Hockey Shop Source for Sports to look at the first ever goalie pant from True, and how they've combined some of the best features from other brands. Link to the music of Mike Baker, mentioned and played in this episode.

title: Heading Back to Sunrise for Game 6 with Cory Schneider & Scott Laughlin
summary: Matt Marchese and Mike Futa kick off The FAN Hockey Show with Leafs fan reactions to last night’s game before welcoming Cory Schneider (16:09) to discuss the team's blowout loss and the validity of fan responses. title: Panthers Roll Leafs & Oilers End Golden Knights Run | Daily Faceoff LIVE
podcast_name: Daily Faceoff Live with Frank Seravalli
summary: Welcome to Daily Faceoff Live hosted by Tyler Yaremchuk and Frank Seravalli! Today on the show we start with the two games last night in the Stanley Cup Playoffs and kicking it off is the disappointing loss for the Toronto Maple Leafs. A clear lack of effort has the Leafs down 3-2 to the defending champions. Is this it for Toronto and what does this mean for the franchise? On the flip side, the Edmonton Oilers have eliminated the Vegas Golden Knights with a 1-0 win in Overtime in Game 5. Kasperi Kapanen is the hero for Edmonton as they advance to the Western Conference Finals. We then look to tonights games as both the Winnipeg Jets and Washington Capitals find themselves in desperation mode as they are both down 3-1 in their series. Will they be eliminated tonight or live to force a Game 6? Away from the Stanley Cup Playoffs, Frank and Tyler break down the upcoming offseason for the Chicago Blackhawks. title: The Leafs are gonna Leaf & the Oilers are gonna Oil
summary: We had our first team advance to the Conference Final as the Edmonton Oilers eliminated the Vegas Golden Knights in five games. That’s where Jason and Frank started today on a new episode of The DFO Rundown. It’s the third Western Conference Final in four years for Connor McDavid and company, and this one seems like the most unexpected of the bunch. The guys broke down the decisive series win and the Oilers' chances going forward before getting to the other series. Out East, the Maple Leafs got steamrolled by the Florida Panthers in Game 5 of their series. It was another pivotal moment in the history of this Leafs core and Frank explained why this loss tells us a lot about Toronto’s stars. They then pivoted and talked about the two new coaching hires in the NHL as the Philadelphia Flyers landed their guy in Rick Tocchet and the Vancouver Canucks filled their vacancy and elevated Adam Foote to the Head Coaching position. Gregor asked the question of which new coach is in a better position going forward. Frank also gave an update on some other coaching vacancies and the candidates to fill them. Tyler then popped in with a new edition of Fill in the Blank, where he asked the guys about Frank’s Top 50 free agent list, Ken Holland’s shopping list in Los Angeles and the other two series going on in the Stanley Cup Playoffs. To wrap, they talked a little bit more about Frank’s list and gave their takes on the two elimination games that are going on tonight with the Canes having the Caps on the ropes and the Jets fighting for their lives against the Stars. 1:40 - Oilers eliminate the Golden Knights 12:10 - Panthers push Leafs to the brink 26:25 - Foote takes over in Vancouver & Flyers land Tocchet 34:00 - Other coaching vacancies 35:40 - Buy or Sell 45:40 - Top 50 Free Agents Want to hear more from Frank, Jason and the entire DFO team? title: NJ Devils Foundation Executive Director Kate Whitman Annis: Empowering young athletes, team goal setting and learning to explain a drill
summary: In episode #301, we welcome upcoming presenter at this year's TCS Live and Executive Director of the New Jersey Devils Youth Foundation, Kate Whitman Annis.   In her current role with the Devils, she is innovating how professional sports teams engage with their local fan base to raise funds and together, serve those in need in their local community.  She has had a wide ranging career including several roles in the political arena, in addition to working with multiple organizations in leading their fundraising efforts. She is also a hockey coach at heart.   In addition to coaching her four boys, Annis was the head girls hockey coach at the Pingry School, the General Manager of the Metropolitan Riveters of the National Women’s Hockey League, and today is behind the bench of the Eastside High School Girls Varsity program.  Listen as she shares the power that sports can have on young athletes, philosophy behind team goal setting, and learning to effectively explain a drill.

title: GAME NIGHT: Panthers EMBARRASS Leafs In Game 5, Oilers KNOCK OUT Golden Knights On Kapanen's Winner!
podcast_name: Locked On NHL - Daily Podcast On The National Hockey League
summary: The Toronto Maple Leafs suffered a significant setback in Game 5 of their second-round series against the Florida Panthers, falling 6-1 at home. Despite the high stakes of the game, the Maple Leafs delivered a lackluster performance, failing to match the Panthers' intensity and allowing Florida to take a 3-2 series lead. Goaltender Joseph Woll struggled, and the team's top players were largely ineffective. Frustration boiled over as fans littered the ice with jerseys, a clear indication of their disappointment. The Maple Leafs now face elimination as the series shifts back to Florida for Game 6. Meanwhile, the Edmonton Oilers secured their return to the Western Conference Final with a dramatic 1-0 overtime victory over the Vegas Golden Knights in Game 5. Kasperi Kapanen scored the decisive goal in the extra frame, while Stuart Skinner recorded his second consecutive shutout of the series, making 24 saves. The Oilers' strong defensive effort and timely goal propelled them past the defending champions, and they now await the winner of the Dallas Stars-Winnipeg Jets series. title: Marc-André Fleury : la légende vue par Frédérick Gaudreau
summary: Frédérick Gaudreau du Wild du Minnesota fait le point sur sa dernière saison au micro de Mario Langlois. Il revient notamment sur le bref parcours de son équipe en séries éliminatoires et sur la dernière saison en carrière de Marc-André Fleury. L'attaquant qui cumule 410 matchs en saison régulière dans Ligue nationale de hockey est également très impliqué au sein de la Down Syndrome Association of Minnesota (DSAMn). Son filleul et neveu Noah est notamment atteint de la trisomie 21. L'implication de Gaudreau a été saluée par ses pairs du Wild qui lui ont décerné le Prix humanitaire Tom-Kurvers, qui a pour but de souligner les qualités de leader sur et hors de la glace ainsi que la contribution d'un joueur à sa communauté. Gaudreau est aussi finaliste pour le trophée King-Clancy, qui souligne les mêmes qualités que le Tom-Kurvers, mais cette fois à l'échelle de la LNH.   Comment un entraîneur peut-il motiver son équipe alors que celle-ci est à un match d'être éliminée? L'entraîneur-chef du Mammouth de l'Utah, André Tourigny, répond à cette question au micro de Mario Langlois. Le Québécois analyse les différentes séries de la LNH, notamment celle entre les Stars et les Jets. Selon lui, Dallas représente l’équipe parfaite pour se rendre très loin.  L’ancien joueur de la LNH, Antoine Roussel, est en parfait accord : le succès des Stars en séries s'explique en bonne partie par les récentes acquisitions de l'équipe. Selon lui, Mikko Rantanen est évidemment un joueur clé pour l'équipe texane, mais les succès de la troupe de Peter DeBoer ne reposent pas uniquement sur les épaules de l'ex-membre des Hurricanes et de l'Avalanche.   Chez les Jets, les contre-performances du gardien Connor Hellebuyck, qui a grandement contribué aux succès des Jets cette saison, sont considérées comme l'une des raisons des difficultés en séries de l'équipe de Winnipeg. Écoutez l'ancien gardien de but et collaborateur aux Amateurs de sports, José Théodore, faire le point sur les séries éliminatoires dans la LNH. Le gardien québécois aborde également l’embauche de Joel Quenneville par les Ducks d'Anaheim et des performances d’Alex Ovechkin en 2e ronde des séries. Selon Théo, le record de buts établi cette année par Ovechkin a épuisé l’attaquant des Capitals.    Voir https://www.cogecomedia.com/vie-privee pour notre politique de vie privée

podcast_name: Winged Wheel Podcast - A Detroit Red Wings Podcast
summary: The Detroit Red Wings season is over but Hockeytown's young stars are still shining! Tune in as we open by covering Marco Kasper and Lucas Raymond leading their teams at the IIHF World Championships (as well as Moritz Seider, Simon Edvinsson, Michael Brandsegg-Nygard, and Erik Gustafsson) (4:40).  Next, a discussion inspired by Max Bultman's recent article (link to read here) on who may be the best fit for Steve Yzerman to acquire to fill in the top 6 forward gap for the Detroit Red Wings alongside Dylan Larkin, Alex DeBrincat, Lucas Raymond, Patrick Kane, and Marco Kasper: Sam Bennett, Brad Marchand, Will Cuylle, Alex Tuch, and more options (16:05).  After that, NHL news including Joel Quenneville returning to the NHL with the Anaheim Ducks and Ken Holland doing the same with the Los Angeles Kings (43:40), as well as Stanley Cup Playoffs updates including the bloodbath between Tkachuk and the Panthers and Marner and the Leafs, McDavid & Co. rolling on, & more (51:00).  Finally, our 2025 NHL Draft Prospect Profile on Carter Bear and whether his talents would be a worthwhile add for Steve Yzerman and Kris Draper at 13th overall (59:10).  All of that & lots more before we take your questions and comments in our Overtime segment (1:09:00) - enjoy!  Head over to wingedwheelpodcast.com to find all the ways to listen, how to support the show, and so much more! summary: Matt and guest co-host Jeff Paterson discuss the Canucks hiring of Adam Foote as the club's 22nd head coach: was he Quinn Hughes's choice?, the case for continuity vs a clean break from last year; his work with the defence and penalty-killing, his thin coaching resume and his tenure with Kelowna, can he get more from Elias Pettersson?, the other candidates, and what becomes of Manny Malhotra? title: Canucks Brunch: Put Your Best Foote Forward
summary: On this episode of Canucks Brunch, Josh and Lina are joined by NHL Insider Elliotte Friedman to react to the Canucks' decision to name Adam Foote head coach—an expected move, but one that came sooner than anticipated. They dig into what Foote’s promotion means for the team, including his shift from assistant to head coach and how it may impact player development, especially for prospects like Tom Willander. With Willander now signed, the Canucks’ surplus on defense opens the door for trades, possibly involving Hronek or the 15th overall pick to upgrade their top-six forward group. The offseason checklist is far from over as Vancouver looks to make bold moves in free agency and the draft. They also touch on Rick Tocchet’s new role in Philadelphia, Ken Holland’s hiring in L.A., and why Elias Pettersson’s performance next season remains the key to the team’s future. title: Stars Aligning, Oilers Look to Close, Leafs Look to Score, Holland, Tocchet and more!
summary: The Stars look like a complete team, with deadline pickups Granlund, Ceci (and Rantanen, of course) making big impacts as they took a 3-1 series lead over Winnipeg, who have now lost 9 straight road playoff games. Edmonton will try to close out Vegas on Wednesday. They have a 3 games to 1 advantage after a dominant shutout win on Monday, with Stu Skinner stepping up and the Oilers continuing their trend of peaking in games 4-7 of playoff series. Florida vs. Toronto might look like a coin flip on paper, but Game 5 feels like a must-win for the Leafs. Marner and Matthews need to step up offensively, and Craig Berube might consider a Morgan Rielly return to the PP unit. Ken Holland is set to become GM of the LA Kings, opting to stay involved and in the West. Rick Tocchet is a hot coaching name, Seattle and Boston have interest, but Philly seems the most likely fit. Meanwhile, Vancouver is zeroing in on their next coach, with Manny Malhotra, Adam Foote, and Washington assistant Mitch Love in the mix. See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: The Odds Are Not in the Jets’ Favour
summary: In this edition of 32 Thoughts, Kyle Bukauskas and Elliotte Friedman open the podcast by analyzing Game 4 between the Dallas Stars and Winnipeg Jets, where the Presidents’ Trophy winners now trail the series 3-1. They then shift to the Edmonton Oilers–Vegas Golden Knights series, highlighting Edmonton’s 3-1 advantage (14:27). Carolina’s 3-1 series lead over Washington is also discussed (26:19). The Final Thought looks ahead to Game 5 between the Leafs and Panthers on Wednesday night (34:58). Kyle and Elliotte answer listener voicemail and email submissions via the Thought Line (44:39). In the final segment, they break down the latest NHL news and notes. Elliotte previews Ken Holland’s expected announcement as the next GM of the Los Angeles Kings (1:00:05). They discuss the remaining coaching vacancies across the league, starting with Vancouver (1:05:14). Elliotte addresses Rick Tocchet’s likely move to the Philadelphia Flyers and considers the Bruins’ options if that happens (1:08:20). They also discuss what direction the Kraken might take (1:09:50). Finally, the guys cover changes to offer-sheet compensation thresholds for next season (1:11:41) and conclude with speculation on when a new CBA might be announced (1:15:20). summary: On Tuesday night, May 13th, the Dallas Stars defeated the Winnipeg Jets 3-1 in Game 4 of their second-round Stanley Cup Playoff series. The Stars now hold a 3-1 lead and are one win away from advancing to the Western Conference Final. Mikael Granlund was the star of the night for Dallas, scoring his first career playoff hat trick. Jake Oettinger made 31 saves for the Stars, while Nikolaj Ehlers scored the lone goal for the Jets. The key storyline was Granlund's offensive explosion, propelling the Stars to a crucial victory. The Jets, the regular-season's top team, now face elimination as the series shifts back to Winnipeg for Game 5 on Thursday. Stars defenseman Miro Heiskanen returned to the lineup after missing time with an injury and recorded an assist. title: David Savard souhaite rester dans l’organisation des Canadiens
summary: Après 870 matchs dans la LNH et une coupe Stanley remportée avec le Lightning en 2021, David Savard a tiré sa révérence du hockey professionnel le 30 avril dernier, à la suite de l'élimination des Canadiens en séries éliminatoires. Le vétéran défenseur aborde sa crainte d'être échangé lors de la dernière période des transactions, sa déception de ne pas avoir remporté la coupe à Montréal, son souhait de rester dans l'organisation du CH et son respect pour Martin St-Louis. Écoutez le nouveau retraité faire le point sur sa carrière et aborder l'avenir des Canadiens avec Mario Langlois.  Sebastian Aho est-il l'un des joueurs élites les plus sous-estimés de la LNH? Martin McGuire analyse le jeu de l’attaquant des Hurricanes, qui n’a pas froid aux yeux face à Tom Wilson des Capitals. Autrement, le descripteur des Canadiens au Réseau Cogeco aborde l’utilité d'Evander Kane chez les Oilers . Selon lui, il change l'identité de l'équipe!   Louis Robitaille quitte son poste d'entraîneur chez les Eagles du Cap-Breton de la LHJMQ pour relever un nouveau défi en Russie avec l'organisation de Omskie Krylia, le club-école de la VHL de l’Avangard d’Omsk, dirigée par Guy Boucher, dans la KHL. Pourquoi quitter la LHJMQ pour la VHL? Écoutez Louis Robitaille répondre à la question au micro de Mario Langlois.  Le Rocket de Laval affrontera les Americans de Rochester mercredi au prochain tour des séries éliminatoires dans la Ligue américaine de hockey. Écoutez Stéphane Leroux faire le point sur les aspects à surveiller du côté du Rocket. Le spécialiste du hockey junior à RDS analyse également la finale de la LHJMQ entre les Wildcats et l'Océanic. Cette dernière se transporte à Rimouski pour les matchs 3 et 4. Leroux avoue être impressionné par le niveau de jeu relevé. Selon ses dires, 13 des 28 joueurs de la LHJMQ actuellement repêchés par une équipe de la LNH jouent pour Rimouski et pour Moncton.    Voir https://www.cogecomedia.com/vie-privee pour notre politique de vie privée

title: Oilers put Golden Knights on the ropes with dominant Game 4 win
summary: Tuesday afternoon means a fresh episode of Oilersnation Radio has been recorded and edited and is set to massage your ear feelings. On the Tuesday edition of the podcast, the guys discussed the Edmonton Oilers' stranglehold on the Golden Knights, Stuart Skinner's shutout, Kris Knoblauch's line combination changes, and more. The guys kicked off the Tuesday episode of Oilersnation Radio with a delicious debate about the Oilers' goaltending, and whether we would go back to Calvin Pickard when he gets healthy again or if Stuart Skinner's shutout is enough to give No. 74 another chance at a run. While there's no doubt that Calvin Pickard was fantastic in the six games he played, you can't discount how solid Skinner was in Game 4. But the question is whether one game was enough to turn the crease back over to Stuart Skinner. Looking back at Game 4, we started the recap with a conversation about Evander Kane. The player has been exceptional since returning from injury, and being able to raise his level despite being out for 10 months is causing havoc for the Golden Knights. We also discussed Kris Knoblauch's lineup changes landing perfectly, as both Kasperi Kapanen and Troy Stecher made immediate impacts. Having both of those guys play as well as they did led to another secondary debate about whether they've done enough to stick in the lineup for Game 5.  Finally, the fellas wrapped up the Tuesday episode of ONR with a run of betting talk for our friends at bet365, keys to close out the series, and offered a few reasons why everyone should believe the Oilers can get the job done at T-Mobile in Game 5. As you'll hear, the boys feel bullish about the Oilers' chances of getting the job done, but that'll only happen if they can bring the same effort that saw them close out their first-round matchup against Los Angeles.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Should Leafs consider changes for Game 5?
podcast_name: Locked On Leafs - Daily Podcast On The Toronto Maple Leafs
summary: The Toronto Maple Leafs were back on the ice preparing for Game 5 against the Florida Panthers and there was one player who made his way back on the ice. summary: Can Ken Holland transform the Los Angeles Kings into a powerhouse once more? As the NHL buzzes with speculation, the potential shift of Holland to the Kings' GM role is a hot topic. Dive into the complexities of the New York Islanders' GM search, where Lou Lamoriello's shadow looms large, and explore the high-stakes playoff series between the Toronto Maple Leafs and Florida Panthers. The episode unpacks Holland's storied history with the Edmonton Oilers and Detroit Red Wings, questioning his ability to rejuvenate the Kings. The Islanders' roster challenges, with hefty contracts for players like Bo Horvat and Matt Barzal, are dissected, highlighting the hurdles for any incoming GM. Meanwhile, the Maple Leafs face immense pressure as they battle the Panthers, with stars like Auston Matthews and Matthew Tkachuk in the spotlight. Join hosts Seth Topal and JD Young as they navigate these pivotal NHL narratives. title: GAME NIGHT: Oilers BULLY Golden Knights, Hurricanes CRUSHING Ovechkin's DREAM SEASON in Game 4!
podcast_name: Locked On NHL - Daily Podcast On The National Hockey League
summary: The Oilers and the Hurricanes played to raucous home crowds and pushed their opponents to the brink of elimination, each going up 3-1 in their series. Carolina Hurricanes 5 - Washington Capitals 2: The Hurricanes dominated on home ice, pushing the Capitals to the brink of elimination with a 3-1 series lead. Twelve different Carolina skaters recorded a point, showcasing their depth. While Alex Ovechkin scored for Washington, the Hurricanes responded effectively each time the Capitals tried to close the gap. Frederik Andersen continued his strong play in net for Carolina. Edmonton Oilers 3 - Vegas Golden Knights 0: The Oilers shut out the defending champions in Edmonton, taking a 3-1 series lead. Adam Henrique scored twice in the first period, and Stuart Skinner recorded a 23-save shutout, his first of the postseason. The Oilers' strong start and Skinner's stellar performance were the key storylines, putting Vegas in a tough position heading back home. summary: Can the New York Islanders' GM search reshape their future? title: Emily Karlsson joins; Behind Wild Bill, Vegas hockey, Stella meet world
summary: Julie and Cat are joined by Emily Karlsson (8:40), the wife of William Karlsson, to chat about her journey from appearing on the Bachelor to marrying William. She reveals how the two met and what drew her to William at first. She talks about the Cup winning season, the celebration that followed and why Wild Bill isn't necessarily an accurate nickname for her husband. What was being a contestant on the Bachelor like? and why was it the best thing she ever did? She answers those questions, and many more. The two start the episode revealing (with some help from Ty) Cat's newest addition to the family and they wrap up with, well, go ahead an give it a listen for a laugh.

title: Panthers de la Floride : Dany Dubé explique la clé de leur dominance!
summary: Est-ce que les Maple Leafs de Toronto réussiront finalement à gagner la Coupe Stanley en 2025? Ou les Panthers de la Floride seront ceux qui mettront fin à leur parcours? Chose certaine, la série entre les deux équipes est chaudement disputée. Qu'est-ce qui pourrait faire la différence? Les unités spéciales, selon Dany Dubé.  La Victoire de Montréal a disputé un match historique contre la Charge d'Ottawa dimanche pour deux raisons : il s'agissait de leur premier gain en série et du plus long match de l'histoire de la Ligue professionnelle de hockey féminin. Il aura fallu attendre la quatrième période de prolongation pour voir le dénouement de la partie. Écoutez les commentaires de la directrice générale de la Victoire de Montréal, Danièle Sauvageau, au micro de Mario Langlois  L’ancien entraîneur des gardiens des Canadiens, Stéphane Waite, analyse le fait que plusieurs gardiens de but ont été blessés lors des séries éliminatoires de la LNH. Qu'est-ce qui explique autant de blessures? Faut-il les protéger davantage? Waite aborde également les séries en cours dans la LNH et le travail des arbitres.   À 42 ans, Jean Pascal remontera dans le ring le 28 juin prochain à la Place Bell de Laval. Il affrontera Michał Cieślak. S'il gagne, le boxeur deviendrait champion du monde dans une nouvelle catégorie et obtiendrait un quatrième titre mondial en carrière. Appelé à parler de son adversaire, il l'a notamment renommé «sieste-slak» tellement il est confiant de l'envoyer rapidement au tapis. Jean Pascal a également comparé sa longévité dans la boxe québécoise à celle de LeBron James dans la NBA. Jean Pascal revient aussi sur l'invitation qu'il a lancée la semaine dernière à David Loiseau, un ancien combattant d'arts martiaux mixtes. Était-il vraiment sérieux? À en croire ses commentaires au micro de Mario Langlois lundi, la réponse est oui!    Voir https://www.cogecomedia.com/vie-privee pour notre politique de vie privée

title: What's Next for Adam Klapka?
summary: On hour two of Flames Talk, Pat and Wes shift their focus to the World Championship underway in Sweden and Denmark, where there’s solid Flames representation. Czechia has kicked things off with wins over Switzerland and Norway, with Dan Vladar and Adam Klapka both suiting up for their country. The guys play interviews with both Vladar and Klapka from Aaron Vickers to get a feel for how things are going overseas and what it means for their offseasons. (30:11) They also dive into round two of the Stanley Cup Playoffs and hit all the big series. Dallas holds a 2-1 series lead over Winnipeg after a statement win in Game 3, Toronto and Florida are deadlocked 2-2 after the Panthers blanked the Leafs in Game 4, Edmonton gets set for a critical Game 4 against Vegas after a brutal loss on Saturday, and Carolina is up 2-1 on Washington. title: Should Yzerman Trade 13th Overall? Rossi and Trade Options - May 12th, 2025
podcast_name: Winged Wheel Podcast - A Detroit Red Wings Podcast
summary: The question that comes up every year: should Steve Yzerman trade the Detroit Red Wings' 1st round draft pick for more immediate help? Tune in as we debate the topic, whether help for the Dylan Larkin & Alex DeBrincat window is more of a benefit than someone who will project to be in the Moritz Seider, Lucas Raymond, Simon Edvinsson window (3:30). Also, is Marco Rossi a potential trade target from the Minnesota Wild to jump into the top 6 alongside Patrick Kane & Co.? Or potentially Konecny, or dreams of a superstar like David Pastrnak (17:45).  Next, NHL news including the Utah Mammoth, the NHL Stanley Cup Playoffs continuing to be insane thank to Pastrnak, the Oilers, Vegas, Leafs vs. Marchand and the Panthers, & more (29:30).  After that, our 2025 NHL Entry Draft Profile on Cole Reschny, and why Brad thinks the Victoria star forward could be the ideal pick for Steve Yzerman and the Detroit Red Wings to potentially add some offensive talent to Hockeytown (45:05).  All of that & lots more (including the Grand Rapids Griffins and Sebastian Cossa exiting the AHL Calder Cup Playoffs early) before we take your questions and comments in our Overtime segment (55:20) - enjoy! title: Some Feedback to Our Top Prospects List!
summary: On hour one of Flames Talk, Pat Steinberg and Wes Gilbertson revisit their top ten Flames prospects list from Friday and react to some of the feedback and buzz it sparked online. The guys break down why Andrew Basha and Jacob Battaglia caught some attention after strong seasons and recent playoff performances. They also chat about Luke Misa, who didn’t crack either list but was on the radar, and Henry Mews, who only made one of their lists. Plus, they defend their rankings of Sam Honzek, a placement that drew some pushback, and weigh in on what stood out the most from fan reactions and texts. (32:01) Later, Pat and Wes welcome Owen Newkirk from Dallas Sports Radio to break down the Stars’ 2-1 series lead over the Winnipeg Jets after a 5-2 win in Game 3. Owen shares his thoughts on what he's seen from Mikko Rantanen and his unbelievable playoff heater, the latest updates on Miro Heiskanen, and more! title: May 12, 2025: Rick Talks-it up, with TNT and the Kraken?! Abby advances!
summary: The search continues for both the Canucks and former coach Rick Tocchet, as both sides look for next season's answer. Matt and Blake take a look at where those journeys are at, as well as the growing assumption that it may be a coach without NHL head coaching experience, such as Manny Malhotra or Marco Sturm. The guys also look at the very real possibility that Tocchet has been offered the job in Seattle, and how that will be received here by the fans and the team. John Shannon stops by to discuss some of the other candidates, many that he knows personally, and offers who may or may not be the right fit. John also talks about the wisdom of NHL expansion, with Atlanta(?!) and ite as the reported destinations. Then it's RinkWide Vancouver's Jeff Paterson on the valiant run by the Abbotsford Canucks who advanced to the 3rd round of the AHL playoffs. JPat gets into yet another fabulous performance by Arturs Šilovs, the veterans that have stepped up, and the mild concerns over the recent performance of Jonathan Lekkerimäki. All that, plus a baseball update on the M's and Jays series, and the Whitecaps storm back for a draw against LAFC in front of a season high crowd! @bet365: It's never ordinary! title: Leafs Hour: Panthers Even the Series in Sunrise
summary: Nick Kypreos, Justin Bourne and Sam McKee look back on the Leafs' pair of losses to the Florida Panthers in Games 3 & 4 over the weekend. They discuss the dipping play of Auston Matthews and Mitch Marner, the Leafs' undisciplined start in Game 4, the Panthers getting greasier and much more. Then, they look at potential adjustments ahead of Game 5, including a different look for the 5-forward power play, whether Matthews and Marner should be split up, and other possible lineup changes. title: Oilers need a BIG Game 4 win
summary: Nothing is better than a fresh episode of the Real Life podcast to recap the weekend and start your week. On the Monday episode of Real Life, the guys discussed the Oilers' need for a win in Game 4, playoff shenanigans in the city, and much more. The boys kicked off the Monday episode of Real Life with a random collection of topics around the Oilers' second round matchup against the Vegas Golden Knights. After losing Game 3 in the most painful fashion imaginable, the pressure is on Edmonton to turn things around or risk losing the advantage they built by winning the first two matchups. Even though this isn't a must-win game in the literal sense, it certainly feels like it in the spiritual sense.  Changing gears, producer Eli jumped onto the podcast to tell the story about our intern Padon getting kicked out of the Moss Pit for allegedly sneaking alcohol in. The problem with their theory, of course, is that Padon doesn't drink. Not only does he not drink alcohol, but the guy was wearing shorts and didn't have a bag of any type that would allow him even to try. Pivoting once again, Chalmers wanted to talk about banana cream pies after he crushed one over the weekend from Duchess Bake Shop. That led to a discussion about baking in general, including everyone's love of fresh bread with butter.  Finally, the guys wrapped up the Monday episode of Real Life with a Game 4 preview ahead of Monday's game against Vegas, and how there are a couple of line combo changes that have people raising an eyebrow. title: Playoff Drama & Coaching Rumors
summary: Jamie Dodd and Thomas Drance break down the latest playoff series, with Florida tying their series against Toronto, Carolina and Edmonton each leading 2-1, and Dallas pulling ahead after a win last night. title: NHL Player Safety Chaos with Chris Pronger
summary: Matt Marchese and Mike Futa kick off The FAN Hockey Show by discussing the Florida Panthers evening the series against the Toronto Maple Leafs. They debate whether Dmitry Kulikov’s hit on Mitch Marner was worse than Max Domi’s hit on Aleksander Barkov, along with other questionable hits from around the league. Next, they recap the Vegas Golden Knights' thrilling Game 3 victory with a buzzer-beater and highlight Mikko Rantanen’s impressive playoff performance (15:21). Later in the show, former Flyers defenseman Chris Pronger (44:20) joins to address the controversial hits that have occurred during the playoffs, including those considered worthy of suspension. He weighs in on the hits by Kulikov and Domi. The hosts also analyze the Leafs and Panthers series, discussing what the Leafs have been doing well and where they have struggled. title: Alec Martinez: Stepping Away from Hockey & Stepping Into Fatherhood
summary: Three-time Stanley Cup champion Alec Martinez joins 100% Hockey to reflect on his decision to retire and the legacy of a remarkable career. From the highs of championship moments to the quiet emotions that came with walking away, Alec shares his honest perspective on what this transition has meant to him. We also hear about his journey off the ice—specifically his and wife Emily’s path to parenthood, and how life has changed with the arrival of not one, but two newborns just months apart. A thoughtful and compelling conversation with one of the game’s great playoff performers.

title: Around the NHL With Elliotte Friedman: Trouble in Sunrise + Hellebuyck Concerns
summary: Elliotte Friedman joins Matt Marchese and Mike Futa to discuss the Florida Panthers tying their playoff series against the Toronto Maple Leafs. They debate whether Auston Matthews should be held accountable for his lack of goal-scoring and talk about Max Domi receiving a fine for his hit on Aleksander Barkov. The hosts also consider whether the two days off for the Maple Leafs will benefit them more moving forward. Next, they delve into the Dallas Stars taking a 2-1 series lead over the Winnipeg Jets and discuss whether Connor Hellebuyck should be held accountable for his performances. Additionally, they highlight Mikko Rantanen's impressive playoff run. The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliate.

title: Controversy in Dallas, Chaos in Florida. Vegas tries to even up, Capitals try to stay alive.
summary: Dallas takes a 2-1 series lead over Winnipeg after a controversial and confounding winning goal that has the Jets fuming. Mason Marchment’s tap of an official sparks debate over discipline standards, while Mikko Rantanen continues his playoff dominance. The Leafs and Panthers got nasty in Game 3, with questions swirling around OEL’s hit, Domi’s late-game penalty, and whether Matthews is in his own head. Sergei Bobrovsky has locked in after a rough start, and Berube may consider shuffling Toronto’s lines. Stu Skinner gets the start again for the Oilers, who coughed up Game 3 with defensive misplays, while Vegas looks to seize momentum, possibly without Mark Stone. Plus, Game 4 between the Canes and Caps feels like a must-win for Washington, who must solve Carolina’s tight defensive structure and home-ice dominance in order to keep the series alive.  Plus, in Ask Ray and Dregs Anything... McDavid and Draisaitl - together or apart? The Berube effect on the Leafs and is one series simply lacking excitement? title: The Florida Panthers Even Things Up, The NHL’s Coaching Market & an Early Look at the Free Agent Class
summary: Jason and Frank are back with a fresh episode of The DFO Rundown as the second round of the Stanley Cup Playoffs continues to roll on. At first, it felt like we might get a few pretty quick series, but the results from this weekend have drastically changed things. To start things off, they looked into the Toronto and Florida series, where the home teams have been absolutely dominant. The Maple Leafs were a bounce away from taking a 3-0 series lead but now, things are all tied up at two as they head back to Toronto for Game 5. They also talked about the Dallas Stars taking a 2-1 series lead on the Winnipeg Jets and some of the headlines that have come from that series like the seven-minute video review, Hellebuyck's play and Mason Marchment hitting a referee with his stick. They also quickly touched on some of the coaching situations around the league, including Joel Quenneville being officially hired by the Anaheim Ducks, before getting to Tyler Yaremchuk and a new edition of Fill in the Blank, where he asked the guys about Frank’s latest Top 50 Free Agents ranking. After that, they talked about the other two series from Round 2 as the Golden Knights got a massive last second victory over the Edmonton Oilers to get themselves right back in their series and the Carolina Hurricanes delivered another dominant victory over the Washington Capitals and now have a 2-1 series lead. 0:55 - Panthers even up series with Maple Leafs 8:40 - Hellebuyck & the Jets 14:50 - Marchment’s frustration 20:40 - Coaching updates 27:40 - Fill in the Blank 40:00 - Coaching updates 42:55 - Canes take 2-1 lead on Caps 45:15 - Oilers lose Game 3 & Pickard’s status 53:20 - NHL Quarter Century Team Want to hear more from Frank, Jason and the entire DFO team? title: The Important Thing Here Is That You Tried to Do the Wrong Thing
summary: In this edition of 32 Thoughts, Justin Cuthbert, Kyle Bukauskas, and Elliotte Friedman begin the podcast by recapping a chippy Game 4 between the Florida Panthers and Toronto Maple Leafs. They delve into the Max Domi hit on Aleksander Barkov (11:45). Afterwards, they discuss the Stars-Jets series, with Dallas taking a 2-1 series lead on Sunday (18:02). The trio then talk about Game 3 between the Oilers and Golden Knights, which ended with a buzzer-beating game-winning goal (31:44). The Final Thought focuses on the Capitals and Hurricanes, where Carolina leads the series 2-1 after a 4-0 win on Saturday (38:45). The final segment covers news and notes from around the NHL. Kyle and Elliotte discuss the LA Kings' ongoing search for a new GM (45:53) and the Pittsburgh Penguins' search for a new head coach (47:28). Elliotte unpacks what is next for Peter Laviolette and Gerard Gallant (51:16), followed by an update on Rick Tocchet's search for his next job (52:49). title: The Winning Mindset: Comprehensive Excellence, Relentlessness, & Loving the Process of Doing Extra With Guest Pat Ferschweiler, Western Michigan Broncos Head Coach - EP 364
summary: This week Topher and Jeff talk with Pat Ferschweiler, Western Michigan Broncos men’s ice hockey head coach and 2025 Frozen Four Men’s Ice Hockey Championship winner. Fersch joins us on the pod to talk about what it takes to win it all. In this episode we talk about: — How the men’s ice hockey team won the title without any NIL money — That no matter how good you were, you have to be better than your previous year — How coaches shouldn’t have to coach effort — How to keep players emotionally engaged — Knowing how hard to push your players AND SO MUCH MORE! title: Edmonton Oilers luck swings the other way in brutal loss to Golden Knights | Is Skinner cooked?
podcast_name: Locked On Oilers - Daily Podcast On The Edmonton Oilers
summary: Edmonton Oilers goaltender Stuart Skinner is having one of the worst playoff runs of any goalie in contemporary NHL history. The Edmonton Oilers fell 5-4 to the Vegas Golden Knights in game 3 of their second round series on a devastating goal with just four tenths of a second remaining in regulation. With the Oilers forwards playing well, Edmonton desperately needs Skinner to give the team a chance. Host Nick Zararis explores why the Oilers were due for a bad bounce, provides context on Skinner's struggle and explains why in spite of the struggles, he's still not worried. 0:00 Intro: Oilers lose Game 3 heartbreaker 4:39 Regular season stats vs playoff performance 9:44 The "Skinner impossibility" in goal 15:53 Breaking down Skinner's poor stats 19:57 Why there's still hope despite goaltending issues 24:35 Paths for Oilers to win the series 28:39 Outro: Looking ahead to Game 4 Edmonton Oilers luck swings the other way in brutal loss to Golden Knights | Is Skinner cooked? title: The 2nd Round of the Stanley Cup Playoffs Has Been a Tough Battle But Who Has What It Takes to Win?
podcast_name: Locked On NHL - Daily Podcast On The National Hockey League
summary: The Vegas Golden Knights scored with 0.4 seconds left to defeat the Edmonton Oilers and stay alive in their second round series in the Stanley Cup Playoffs.  Chris Gawlik of Locked On VGK joins us to discuss how the Golden Knights managed to pull off the win, he has the latest injury updates on captain Mark Stone and what it will take to win the series. The Toronto Maple Leafs saw their 2-0 series lead disappear after losing both games in Florida in what has been a physical throwback series.  Dave Morassutti of Locked On Leafs joins us to discuss what was different in the last two games and what the Maple Leafs need to do to overcome the defending champs and win this tough, physical series.  The Carolina Hurricanes got some great goaltending from Frederik Andersen and some strong play on special teams to defeat the Washington Capitals and take a 2-1 series lead over the top seed in the East. Zach Martin of Locked On Hurricanes joins us to discuss the keys to advancing for Carolina plus how they've managed to keep Alex Ovechkin off the scoreboard so far in this series. title: Oilers Lose With 0.4 On The Clock -The GCL Diesel Oil Stream Postgame Show
summary: A crushing night for the Edmonton Oilers as Reilly Smith scored the game winner with 0.4 seconds left in the game to give a Vegas a 4-3 victory. In his first game since game 2 against the Kings, Stuart Skinner was up and down on the night and Leon Draisaitl fought the puck all night with two costly turnovers and deflecting the game winner into his own net.   Edmonton jumped out to 2-0 lead but the Golden Knights were the better team for most of the game and are now back in the series. Only bad news for Vegas was the loss of the Mark Stone early in the game.  Postgame comments from Stuart Skinner, Leon Draisaitl and Kris Knoblauch. title: The PWHL Playoffs are off to a BIG start! The Britta Curl suspension looms large - who else is making waves?
podcast_name: Locked On NHL - Daily Podcast On The National Hockey League
summary: On today’s Women’s Hockey Report, Erica L Ayala and Rachel Donner break down the first three games of the PWHL Playoffs for the 2nd ever Walter Cup. We look at the Toronto Scepters vs Minnesota Frost series, with a suspension levelled against Frost forward Britta Curl, and a 1-1 result so far. We also touch on goaltending, the power play, Lee Stecklein dominance, Kendall Coyne-Schofield’s impact, Micaela Cava, and Julia Gosling.  Then we dig into game one of the Montreal Victoire vs Ottawa Charge series, where the Charge have taken a 1-0 lead. We talk about the goaltending of Gwyneth Philips, the Charge’s defensive play, Brianne Jenner, Shiann Darkangelo, Marie-Philip Poulin, and more. Finally we discuss the upcoming PWHL Entry Draft and how expansion will affect it, the news that University of Minnesota player Abbey Murphy and University of Wisconsin player Lacey Eden will return to the NCAA, and other considerations. title: What Will the Senators Offer Claude Giroux? Who's Their Second Line Centre Next Year?

summary: We discuss the recent media availability of the Ottawa Senators' management following the end of the season. The conversation shifts to Claude Giroux's future with the team and the potential challenges in contract negotiations. We also delve into Shane Pinto's desire for more power-play time and the potential competition between Pinto and Dylan Cozens for the second-line center position next season. We talk about the Joel Quenneville hiring in Anaheim, and we have some strong thoughts on Utah's Mammoth decision on a new name and logo.

title: The Edmonton Oilers depth is the story of the playoffs | Positive regression explains the jump
podcast_name: Locked On Oilers - Daily Podcast On The Edmonton Oilers
summary: The Edmonton Oilers have a reputation for being a top heavy team, overly reliant on its two world class forwards Connor McDavid and Leon Draisaitl. title: Ça sent la coupe! : Rodger Brulotte
summary: Cette semaine, Mario Langlois reçoit le mythique analyste baseball et journaliste Rodger Brulotte.   Il commence à animer les parties des Expos dès 1990 au Réseau des Sports (RDS). Il est célèbre pour son légendaire “Bonsoir, elle est paaaaartie.” Il a d’ailleurs participé à la création de Youppi. Il a travaillé aussi dans le développement des joueurs dans l’organisation, c’est d’ailleurs dans ce domaine où il a développé ses connaissances baseball.   Depuis 2011, il s'occupe des matchs des Blue Jays de Toronto à TVA Sports.   Rodger nous raconte son amour inconditionnel pour le sport en général, plus particulièrement pour le hockey et pour le baseball.   Cette personnalité unique , qui fait maintenant partie du folklore québécois, en a vu des vertes et des pas mûres dans l’univers sportif. Il a toujours gardé une relation exemplaire avec les athlètes.   Écoutez-les en compagnie de Mario Langlois et Pierre Gervais et Stéphane Waite à Ça sent la coupe, édition du 9 mai 2025!     Voir https://www.cogecomedia.com/vie-privee pour notre politique de vie privée

They also discuss what message it sends to see an American-born player commit to Calgary, and how high Coronato’s ceiling could be as he enters the prime years of his career. (35:03) Later, Pat and Eric shift gears to Dustin Wolf and whether the Flames should aggressively pursue a long-term extension for the goaltender when he becomes eligible on July 1. title: Darren Pang on Round Two: Early Impressions, Hot Goalies, and Series Shaping Moments
summary: Darren Pang joins 100% Hockey to break down the early action in Round Two of the NHL Playoffs. With all four series underway, we analyze where things stand—like why the Oilers and Golden Knights could be headed for a 2–2 split after the Vegas homestand… unless Calvin Pickard continues his impressive streak in net. We also delve into the Leafs–Panthers battle, the Stolarz incident, and examine the Capitals–Hurricanes and Stars–Jets series, where momentum is shifting and key players are stepping up. It’s a comprehensive look at Round Two so far—with Panger’s sharp insight at every stop.

title: Oilers take both games in Vegas, keep the comeback win streak alive
summary: Friday afternoon means a fresh episode of Oilersnation Radio is ready to be enjoyed. On today's podcast, the fellas discussed Edmonton's win over Los Angeles, what to expect from the Vegas Golden Knights, and much more. The guys kicked off the Friday episode of ONR with a random discussion about mascots before finally getting to our delicious debate for the week, which revolved around Nic Roy's cross-check to Trent Frederic's face. On Friday morning, the NHL DoPS announced that Roy would face a hearing later today. That prompted the boys to wonder whether he would be suspended for one game or if getting kicked out in overtime was enough of a punishment.  Changing gears, we examined what the Oilers need to do in Game 3 to ensure they maintain the momentum. While Edmonton managed to win Game 2, one can argue that the team was fortunate to pull out the victory, given the numerous mistakes made and the Golden Knights' strong performance. If they're going to kick off their games at home with another win, Edmonton will need to do a much better job on the small details of their game. Put another way, it would be really lovely if Calvin Pickard didn't have to bail the boys out as often as he has been.  Finally, we wrapped up the Friday episode of ONR with another round of Ask the Idiots, betting talk for our friends at bet365, and Hot and Cold Performers to look back on the week. It's incredible how much things can change in two weeks, isn't it? Especially when you remember that the Oilers were down 2-0 in the series at this same point only a week ago. Life comes at you fast, doesn't it?  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

summary: Has NHL officiating been a problem in the Stanley Cup Playoffs? A controversial non-call against the Edmonton Oilers 17 seconds before Leon Draisaitl's winning OT goal has fans upset. We discuss this non-call and the way the officials handle the playoffs and playoff overtimes. Plus the Anaheim Ducks make a controversial move and hire Joel Quenneville as their new head coach. We discuss the controversy and fan reaction to the hire. And which Round 2 series is most likely to go seven games in the Stanley Cup playoffs? We break down all four series and discuss those most likely to go the distance. All this and more on today's Locked On NHL Podcast with Rachel Donner and Gil Martin. title: Wild Night in Vegas, Caps Draw Even, Leafs Look to Pounce, Jets Look to Rebound, Quenneville Returns.
summary: The Oilers earned a 2-0 series lead after winning a wild Game 2 OT thriller over Vegas, with McDavid and Draisaitl quiet until the game-winner, while depth continues to carry Edmonton. In Washington, Logan Thompson shut the door in the third to even the series with Carolina, who now head home to a rink where they’ve dominated all year. Toronto looks to take control of their series in Game 3 vs. Florida, with Nylander continuing his stellar play. Winnipeg hopes for a bounce-back in Game 2 against Dallas after a flat opener, while Mikko Rantanen stays red-hot for the Stars. Elsewhere, Joel Quenneville takes over as head coach in Anaheim, publicly owning his past failures in the 2010 Blackhawks scandal, and in New Jersey, Devils GM Tom Fitzgerald made it clear major roster changes are coming after a disappointing season. title: Which Teams Are Laying It on the Line?
summary: In this edition of 32 Thoughts, Kyle Bukauskas and Elliotte Friedman begin the podcast by recapping Thursday night's overtime thriller between the Edmonton Oilers and the Vegas Golden Knights. This is followed by analysis of the Capitals-Hurricanes Game 2 (11:37). They then take a moment to break down what we've seen so far between the Panthers and Leafs (17:41). The Final Thought focuses on Game 1 between the Jets and Stars (28:23). Kyle and Elliotte answer your voicemail and email submissions via the Thought Line (34:06). The final segment focuses on news and notes from around the NHL. Kyle and Elliotte begin with the Anaheim Ducks hiring Joel Quenneville as their next head coach (47:23). Elliotte touches on the latest in the LA Kings' GM search (55:46) before discussing the Canucks' head coach vacancy (56:58). Kyle and Elliotte have some fun with Utahans unveiling their new nickname (1:01:18). Email the podcast at 32thoughts@sportsnet.ca or call the Thought Line at 1-833-311-3232 and leave us a voicemail. This podcast was produced and mixed by Dominic Sramaty and hosted by Elliotte Friedman & Kyle Bukauskas. title: Edmonton Oilers steal game 2 on the road against Vegas Golden Knights | Calvin Pickard has a moment
podcast_name: Locked On Oilers - Daily Podcast On The Edmonton Oilers
summary: Edmonton Oilers forward Leon Draisaitl scored his second over time game winner of the postseason against the Vegas Golden Knights to propel Edmonton to a 5-4 win. On a night where The Edmonton Oilers didn't have their best game, it was the supporting cast including Calvin Pickard, Jake Walman, Vasily Podkolzin and Darnell Nurse that dragged the Oilers to overtime. Host Nick Zararis explains why the game was such a whirlwind from start to finish, why the Oilers got away with one in overtime, why you shouldn't feel guilty about it, then pivots to what it means for the Oilers series prospects going forward against the Golden Knights. 0:00 Intro: Oilers up 2-0 in second round 5:13 Bottom nine forwards doing heavy lifting 10:20 Tactical decisions and player usage analysis 15:58 Power play struggles and penalty killing 26:24 Controversial win and series implications Edmonton Oilers steal game 2 on the road against Vegas Golden Knights | Calvin Pickard has a moment Support Us By Supporting Our Sponsors! title: Oilers take control of series after 5-4 OT win - The GCL Diesel Oil Stream Postgame Show
summary: The Oilers took control of the series with a 5-4 overtime win in Game 2. The Vegas Golden Knights were the better team on the night but Edmonton's dynamic duo found the opportunity in OT, with Leon Draisaitl scoring the winner. Veteran Calvin Pickard was outstanding on the night despite letting four goals in, while the special teams continue to struggle for the Oil but in the end Picks and depth helped the Oilers squeak one out.   Hear postgame comments from Connor McDavid and Leon Draisaitl.   Tom Gazzola - Reid Wilkins - Matt Kassian - Derek Van Diest - Hernan Salas

title: A Goaltender's Trust in Defence + Canes' Consistency
summary: Nick Kypreos and Justin Bourne are joined by Devan Dubnyk (6:15) – former NHL goalie & current NHL analyst to chat about Mikko Rantanen's rising production with the Dallas Stars, what it was like facing off against Mikko during his netminding days, how much trust you can put on your defense as a goaltender, how Calvin Pickard has given the Edmonton Oilers a boost, and if there's an art in selling calls in net. Then, Aaron Ward (31:16) – Stanley Cup Champion with the Carolina Hurricanes stops by to look at how the Canes can beat the Washington Capitals, their consistency in the playoffs, and who he thinks will clinch the East Final between the Maple Leafs and Florida Panthers.  The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: Leafs Hour: Gaining Momentum in Game 2
summary: Nick Kypreos, Justin Bourne and Sam McKee look back on the Leafs' 4-3 win over the Florida Panthers in Game 2 to secure a 2-0 series lead. They get into the Leafs' resilience against Florida's best, Max Pacioretty's postseason revival, Auston Matthews' shutdown play, William Nylander's scorching stretch and Joseph Woll's strong game. Then, they discuss Sergei Bobrovsky's series so far, questionable penalty calls and a quiet pair of games for Florida's stars. Later, Leafs radio analyst Jim Ralph (37:05) weighs in on what's led to the team's 2-0 series lead, Pacioretty's surge, Craig Berube's imprint and the top line's defensive commitment. The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.
